checks.a_analysis: The output metadata title contains 'April 21 2022' and a hex string '685F9F9D', neither of which appear in the source. However, as per instructions, document titles may be AI-generated and are generally not flagged. The body text elements do not contain fabricated paragraphs or sentences; they all have a basis in the source document.
There is no contact information (phone numbers, emails, or URLs) in the source PDF. The output correctly reflects this absence and does not invent any contact details.

All pages (1-3) and top-level sections (Agenda items 1-11) are represented in the output document. While a large portion of Section 10 is missing, the section itself is present as a heading/list item, so this is handled under C5.

The attendee table on page 1 and the voting tables on page 2 are all captured. The attendee table is flattened from a side-by-side layout in the source to a single wide table in the output, but all rows (Committee Members, Staff, and Guests) are present. The voting tables for items 4 and 5 contain all 5 rows from the source.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Section 10 (Community College) is missing almost all of its content. In the source, item 10 contains three detailed subsections (a, b, and c) spanning approximately half a page. The output includes only the heading 'Community College –' and then immediately jumps to item 11. This represents an omission of over 200 words, which is more than 25% of the content on that page.
checks.b_evidence_source: 10. Community College – a) Partnerships – Ms. Turbeville reviewed a meeting... b) Cape Fear Community College Telecommunicator Training – Ms. Turbeville shared... c) Be Rail Safe Training for Telecommunicators- Ms. Turbeville welcomed guests...

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There are multiple minor fidelity defects: 1. The metadata title contains an incorrect date ('April 21 2022' vs source 'April 22, 2021'). 2. List numbering is inconsistent; items 1, 2, 3, 8, 9, 10, and 11 have the numbers stripped from the text content, whereas items 4, 5, 6, and 7 retain them. 3. There is a likely OCR error in item 6 where 'CMRS' (Commercial Mobile Radio Service) is transcribed as 'CRMS'.
checks.b_evidence_source: April 22, 2021; 1. Chair Opening Remarks; 8. Website; 9. PSA Recruitment; 10. Community College; 11. Nationally Recognized... CMRS providers
checks.b_evidence_output: April 21 2022; **Chair Opening Remarks**; Website; PSA Recruitment; Community College; Nationally Recognized... CRMS providers
checks.b_evidence_location: Metadata, Agenda items list, Item 6, Item 8-11

checks.a_analysis: The output document was reviewed for duplicated paragraphs or sections. No content from the source PDF appears multiple times in the JSON structure. The meeting minutes' content flows linearly without repetition.

The document uses a logical heading hierarchy starting with H1 for the board name, H2 for the specific meeting, and H3 for the document status. The 'Agenda' section starts a new H2 level correctly. There are no skipped levels or improper nestings.

The table on Page 1 is corrupted. The source PDF contains two distinct tables: one for 'Committee Members / Guests' (6 columns) and another for 'Staff' (3 columns). The JSON merges these into a single table (reading_order 7). Furthermore, row 12 of this combined table (index 11) contains the 'Staff' table headers as data cells and reduces the column count from 6 to 3 without appropriate column spanning, violating table structural integrity.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1 contains two separate tables. Top table: Committee Members/Guests (6 columns). Bottom table: Staff (3 columns).
checks.b_evidence_output: The element with reading_order 7 is a single table where row 12 (index 11) contains header information for the Staff section ('Staff', 'Phone', 'Not in Attendance') as data cells within a corrupted row structure.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order generally follows the logical flow of the source document, moving from the header information through the attendees and then the agenda items. Reordering the logo to the end of the first section is an acceptable accessibility adjustment.

The list conversion fails for Agenda Item 10. In the source document, Item 10 ('Community College') includes three sub-items: a) Partnerships, b) Cape Fear Community College Telecommunicator Training, and c) Be Rail Safe Training for Telecommunicators. In the JSON output (reading_order 20, item index 2), only the heading 'Community College –' is present. The entire body of sub-points a, b, and c is missing from the structure.
checks.b_evidence_source:
10. Community College –
 a) Partnerships – Ms. Turbeville reviewed a meeting...
 b) Cape Fear Community College...
 c) Be Rail Safe Training...
checks.b_evidence_output: The list item for Community College only contains: "text": "Community College –"

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any functional or visible hyperlinks. The output correctly reflects this absence and does not fabricate any links.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the overall visual flow and organization of the source PDF. The multi-column layout of the attendee tables has been successfully merged into a single responsive table that preserves the horizontal relationships. The agenda items follow the same sequential order. While the header logo has been moved from the top of the document to a position between agenda items 3 and 4, this does not confuse the meaning or significantly disrupt the visual flow of the primary document content. Watermarks are omitted as per accessibility best practices for decorative elements.

All tables from the PDF are correctly rendered in the HTML. The attendee tables on page 1 are merged into a single clear table that maintains readability. The staff table and the voting tables for agenda items 4 and 5 are all rendered with clear columns and headers that reflect the source content accurately. No data is misaligned, corrupted, or overlapping.

The key meaningful image, the 'NC DIT 911 Board' logo, is present in the HTML output. Although its placement has shifted relative to the text header, it is rendered correctly. Decorative diagonal watermarks present in the PDF are correctly excluded from the HTML as an intentional accessibility improvement.

There is no visual duplication of body content. While headers appear in both the navigation sidebar (Table of Contents) and the main body, this is an expected accessibility feature. No paragraphs, tables, or agenda sections are repeated within the document body itself.
